# NSF SBIR Phase I -- AI/ML for Education

## Overview

The National Science Foundation Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase I program provides up to $275,000 for early-stage technology R&D. This opportunity aligns with the room's problem domain (AI-powered personalized learning) and team profile (US small business, pre-revenue).

## Relevance to Room

- **Problem-definition:** Grant focus on AI/ML innovation directly supports the personalized learning pathway technology
- **Financial-model:** Non-dilutive funding covers 6-9 months of MVP development without equity dilution
- **Team-execution:** SBIR requires US small business status, which matches team profile

## Key Requirements

- US small business (fewer than 500 employees)
- Principal Investigator employed by the company
- Technology must demonstrate innovation and commercial potential
- 6-month Phase I project period

## Next Steps

Promote to funding pipeline for detailed eligibility research and application preparation.
